title: Performance of `:key` for non-reactive data
body:
Is there a performance overhead associated with setting `:key` if I know the data I'm iterating is non-reactive/never going to change?

If there is, is it negligible enough to always set `:key` as a best practice (which I believe is currently generally recommended)?
author: WilliamVenner

title: Adding interactive diagrams to the docs
body:
Hey everyone, 

I recently shared an interactive diagram of the vuejs code base on reddit (r/vuejs) and got a surprisingly positive response, so I thought I would reach out here. 

I generated it using [CodeBoarding](https://github.com/CodeBoarding/CodeBoarding), an open-source tool I built that generates interactive architecture diagrams (static analysis + LLM) to help contributors and users navigate Python repos.

I believe this would be a useful addition to the existing docs. Please see the diagram below and see if you agree:
https://github.com/CodeBoarding/GeneratedOnBoardings/blob/main/vue/on_boarding.md

Let me know if you are interested in adding this to `vue` I would happily make a PR! Many thanks.
author: brovatten

title: 🐛 v-html Breaks SSR on Page Refresh - Hydration Mismatch
body:
Using v-html in Nuxt components causes hydration mismatches that break pages when refreshed. The issue occurs because:
Server-Side: Nuxt renders static HTML during SSR
Client-Side: Vue processes v-html and potentially changes DOM structure
Mismatch: Vue detects differences and discards server HTML, causing content to break

<ClientOnly> - Defeats SSR purpose, bad for SEO

1. Is there a native Vue/Nuxt solution for safe HTML rendering in SSR?
2. Should v-html behavior be consistent between server and client?
author: Zisan-uzum

title: Vue Vapor & Vue runtime?
body: My understanding, which might not be right, is when using vapor mode at the app level you are going to still be required to have the vue runtime. If your using `createVaporApp` shouldn't you not need the vue runtime at all since you will not need the flexibility to use VDOM & Signals? Maybe this is a temp measure & in the end you won't need the vue runtime?
author: Ncage1974

title: Vue's stance on scoped element registries?
body:
Given that Safari added support for scoped custom elements as of [26.0](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/safari-release-notes/safari-26-release-notes#New-Features) and Blink [intends to ship](https://groups.google.com/a/chromium.org/g/blink-dev/c/mAteNymnc_s/m/3donjH1CBwAJ). 

We will soon have access to scoped element registries. This is a powerful tool for web component authors but it will not be as powerful unless we have framework support. It would be really nice to get a gauge of how Vue views scoped element registries.
author: filimon-danopoulos

title: Vue 3 Gantt Component with Dedicated Resource View & Overload Detection (MIT)
body:
Hi Vue community 

I’d like to share a feature I recently introduced in my open-source project **[Jordium Gantt Vue3](https://github.com/nelson820125/jordium-gantt-vue3)** — a **dedicated Resource View mode** for Vue 3 applications.

This component is fully MIT licensed and built natively with Vue 3 + TypeScript.

While most Gantt components are task-centric, this version supports switching between:

- **Task View (traditional Gantt layout)**
- **Resource View (resource-oriented scheduling)**

---

## What Makes the Resource View Different?

Instead of rendering rows as tasks, **rows represent resources**.

Tasks are then rendered under each assigned resource based on allocation percentage.

### Core Capabilities
#### 1. Percentage-Based Allocation

Each task can assign multiple resources:
``` typescript
resources: [
  { resourceId: 'devA', percent: 50 },
  { resourceId: 'devB', percent: 30 }
]
```

This allows:

- Multi-resource assignment
- Partial workload distribution
- Flexible modeling of real-world scheduling

---

#### 2. Resource Overload Detection

If overlapping tasks assigned to the same resource exceed 100% allocation:

- The UI visually highlights the overload
- Helps detect conflicts immediately
- Useful for internal scheduling dashboards

This is handled reactively without introducing a heavy scheduling engine.

---

#### 3. Shared Rendering Core

Both Task View and Resource View share the same rendering pipeline.

The abstraction layer switches only at the row-model level:

- Task rows → grouped by task hierarchy
- Resource rows → grouped by resource

This avoids duplicating layout logic while keeping the architecture maintainable.

If anyone has suggestions on improving this row abstraction pattern in Vue 3, I’d love feedback.

---

## Technical Highlights

- Vue 3 + Composition API
- Fully TypeScript
- Virtual scrolling for large datasets
- Drag / resize support
- Dependency lines & milestones
- Slot-based extensibility
- MIT licensed

---

## Design Philosophy

The goal is not to build a monolithic enterprise scheduling engine.

Instead, the aim is:

- A modern Vue-native foundation
- Flexible data-driven rendering
- Resource-aware visualization without enforcing scheduling rules
- Suitable for ERP dashboards, admin tools, internal planning systems

---

## Looking for Feedback On

- Resource View UX patterns
- Allocation modeling approaches
- Performance considerations for large resource sets
- API design for switching views
- Extensibility patterns

Appreciate any architectural or design feedback.
author: nelson820125

title: ORCH — CLI orchestrator for AI agent teams (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor)
body:
Hey community! 👋

I built **[ORCH](https://github.com/oxgeneral/ORCH)** — an open-source CLI orchestrator that coordinates teams of AI coding agents from the terminal.

If you use Claude Code, Codex, or Cursor to build your projects, ORCH coordinates them so they work as a team:

```bash
npm install -g @oxgeneral/orch

orch agent add "architect" --adapter claude --model claude-opus-4-6
orch agent add "coder" --adapter codex

orch task add "Design new component" -p 1 --assignee architect
orch task add "Implement component" -p 2 --assignee coder --scope "src/components/**"

orch tui  # live TUI dashboard
```

## Key features

- **State machine** — `todo → in_progress → review → done` with auto-retry
- **Scope locking** — no file conflicts between concurrent agents
- **Inter-agent messaging** — agents share context automatically
- **Zero infrastructure** — plain YAML/JSON, no database

**1,657 tests · TypeScript strict · MIT · https://github.com/oxgeneral/ORCH**

Happy to discuss! 🚀
author: oxgeneral
